utilities

Interface StackADT<E>

  • Type Parameters:
    E - The type of elements held in this stack.
    All Known Implementing Classes:
    MyStack

    public interface StackADT<E>
    The StackADT interface defines the abstract data type for a stack data structure to be used in CPRG304 at SAIT Polytechnic.

    A stack follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle, meaning the last element pushed onto the stack is the first one to be popped off. This interface provides all standard stack operations as well as several helper methods for additional flexibility.

    Implementations of this interface must not use any classes from java.util.* collection framework as the underlying data structure.

    Version:
    1.0
    Author:
    Vansh

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void clear()
      Removes all elements from this stack.
      boolean contains(E toFind)
      Returns true if this stack contains the specified element.
      boolean equals(StackADT<E> that)
      Compares the specified StackADT with this stack for equality.
      boolean isEmpty()
      Returns true if this stack co ntains no elements.
      Iterator<E> iterator()
      Returns an iterator over the elements in this stack in proper sequence, from top to bottom.
      E peek()
      Returns the element at the top of this stack without removing it.
      E pop()
      Removes and returns the element at the top of this stack.
      void push(E toAdd)
      Pushes the specified element onto the top of this stack.
      int search(E toFind)
      Returns the 1-based position from the top of the stack where the specified element is located.
      int size()
      Returns the number of elements currently in this stack.
      boolean stackOverflow()
      Returns true if the number of elements in the stack equals its fixed maximum capacity.
      java.lang.Object[] toArray()
      Returns an Object array containing all elements in this stack in proper sequence from bottom to top.
      E[] toArray(E[] toHold)
      Returns an array containing all elements in this stack in proper sequence.

    • Method Detail

      • push

        void push(E toAdd)
   throws java.lang.NullPointerException
        Pushes the specified element onto the top of this stack.

        Pre-condition: toAdd must not be null.

        Post-condition: The element is placed on top of the stack and the stack size increases by one.

        Parameters:
        toAdd - The element to be pushed onto the top of the stack.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - If the specified element is null.

      • pop

        E pop()
throws java.util.EmptyStackException
        Removes and returns the element at the top of this stack.

        Pre-condition: The stack must not be empty.

        Post-condition: The top element is removed and the stack size decreases by one.

        Returns:
        The element removed from the top of the stack.
        Throws:
        java.util.EmptyStackException - If the stack contains no elements.

      • peek

        E peek()
throws java.util.EmptyStackException
        Returns the element at the top of this stack without removing it.

        Pre-condition: The stack must not be empty.

        Post-condition: The stack remains unchanged.

        Returns:
        The element at the top of the stack.
        Throws:
        java.util.EmptyStackException - If the stack contains no elements.

      • clear

        void clear()
        Removes all elements from this stack.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: The stack is empty and its size is zero.

      • isEmpty

        boolean isEmpty()
        Returns true if this stack co ntains no elements.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: The stack remains unchanged.

        Returns:
        true if the stack is empty; false otherwise.

      • toArray

        java.lang.Object[] toArray()
        Returns an Object array containing all elements in this stack in proper sequence from bottom to top.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: The stack remains unchanged.

        Returns:
        An array containing all elements of this stack in proper sequence.

      • toArray

        E[] toArray(E[] toHold)
     throws java.lang.NullPointerException
        Returns an array containing all elements in this stack in proper sequence. The runtime type of the returned array is that of the specified array. If the stack fits in the specified array, it is returned therein; otherwise, a new array of the same runtime type is allocated.

        Pre-condition: The specified array must not be null.

        Post-condition: The stack remains unchanged.

        Parameters:
        toHold - The array into which the elements of this stack are to be stored, if it is big enough; otherwise, a new array of the same runtime type is allocated for this purpose.
        Returns:
        An array containing the elements of this stack.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - If the specified array is null.

      • contains

        boolean contains(E toFind)
          throws java.lang.NullPointerException
        Returns true if this stack contains the specified element. More formally, returns true if and only if this stack contains at least one element e such that toFind.equals(e).

        Pre-condition: toFind must not be null.

        Post-condition: The stack remains unchanged.

        Parameters:
        toFind - The element whose presence in this stack is to be tested.
        Returns:
        true if this stack contains the specified element; false otherwise.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - If the specified element is null.

      • search

        int search(E toFind)
        Returns the 1-based position from the top of the stack where the specified element is located. The topmost element on the stack is considered to be at distance 1. The equals method is used to compare toFind to the elements in this stack.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: The stack remains unchanged.

        Parameters:
        toFind - The element to search for in this stack.
        Returns:
        The 1-based position from the top of the stack where the element is located, or -1 if the element is not on the stack.

      • iterator

        Iterator<E> iterator()
        Returns an iterator over the elements in this stack in proper sequence, from top to bottom.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: The stack remains unchanged.

        Returns:
        An Iterator over the elements in this stack from top to bottom.

      • equals

        boolean equals(StackADT<E> that)
        Compares the specified StackADT with this stack for equality. Two stacks are considered equal if they contain equal elements appearing in the same order from top to bottom.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: Both stacks remain unchanged.

        Parameters:
        that - The StackADT to be compared to this stack.
        Returns:
        true if the two stacks are equal; false otherwise.

      • size

        int size()
        Returns the number of elements currently in this stack.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: The stack remains unchanged.

        Returns:
        The current number of elements in the stack as an integer.

      • stackOverflow

        boolean stackOverflow()
        Returns true if the number of elements in the stack equals its fixed maximum capacity. This method is only meaningful when a fixed-size stack is required; dynamically sized implementations should always return false.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: The stack remains unchanged.

        Returns:
        true if the stack is at capacity; false otherwise.
